results.question: List the id, first name and last name of the customers who both have placed more than 2 orders and have bought at least 3 items.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id ,  T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(*)  >  2 INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id ,  T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id JOIN Order_items AS T3 ON T2.order_id  =  T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(*)  >= 3	e_commerce
results.baseline_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id , T1.customer_first_name , T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 2 AND COUNT(T3.order_item_id) > 3
results.decomposition_plan:
1. Tables: Customers, Orders, Order_Items
2. Join path: Customers -> Orders -> Order_Items
3. Filters: 
   - Orders: order_status_code = 'placed' (assuming placed status means order is confirmed)
   - Order_Items: order_item_status_code = 'shipped' (assuming shipped status means item is delivered)
   - Group by: customer_id
   - Having: count(Orders) > 2 and count(Order_Items) > 2
4. Aggregation / grouping: count(Orders), count(Order_Items)
5. Ordering / limit: None
6. Subqueries / set operations: None
results.decomposition_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id , T1.customer_first_name , T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id WHERE T2.order_status_code = 'placed' AND T3.order_item_status_code = 'shipped' GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 2 AND COUNT(T3.order_item_id) > 2

results.question: What are the ids, first and last names of the customers who have ordered more than twice and have bought at least 3 items?
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id ,  T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(*)  >  2 INTERSECT SELECT T1.customer_id ,  T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id JOIN Order_items AS T3 ON T2.order_id  =  T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING count(*)  >= 3	e_commerce
results.baseline_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 2 AND COUNT(T3.order_item_id) >= 3
results.decomposition_plan:
1. Tables: 
   - Customers
   - Orders
   - Order_Items

2. Join path: 
   - Customers
   - Orders (on customer_id)
   - Order_Items (on order_id)

3. Filters: 
   - Orders.count > 2
   - Order_Items.count >= 3

4. Aggregation / grouping: 
   - Group by Customers.customer_id, Customers.customer_first_name, Customers.customer_last_name

5. Ordering / limit: 
   - None

6. Subqueries / set operations: 
   - None
results.decomposition_sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T1.customer_first_name, T1.customer_last_name HAVING COUNT(T2.order_id) > 2 AND COUNT(T3.order_item_id) >= 3

results.question: Which customers did not make any orders? List the first name, middle initial and last name.
results.gold_sql: SELECT customer_first_name ,  customer_middle_initial ,  customer_last_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_middle_initial ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id	e_commerce
results.baseline_sql: SELECT customer_first_name , customer_middle_initial , customer_last_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Orders)
results.decomposition_plan:
1. Tables: Customers
2. Join path: Left join with Orders
3. Filters: Orders.order_id is null
4. Aggregation / grouping: None
5. Ordering / limit: None
6. Subqueries / set operations: None
results.decomposition_sql: SELECT customer_first_name , customer_middle_initial , customer_last_name FROM Customers LEFT JOIN Orders ON Customers.customer_id = Orders.customer_id WHERE Orders.order_id IS NULL

results.question: WHat are the first and last names, and middle initials of all customers who did not make any orders?
results.gold_sql: SELECT customer_first_name ,  customer_middle_initial ,  customer_last_name FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT T1.customer_first_name ,  T1.customer_middle_initial ,  T1.customer_last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id  =  T2.customer_id	e_commerce
results.baseline_sql: SELECT customer_first_name , customer_last_name , customer_middle_initial FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Orders)
results.decomposition_plan:
1. Tables: Customers
2. Join path: None (no join needed)
3. Filters: Orders.customer_id IS NULL
4. Aggregation / grouping: None (no aggregation needed)
5. Ordering / limit: None (no ordering or limiting needed)
6. Subqueries / set operations: None (no subquery or set operation needed)
results.decomposition_sql: SELECT customer_first_name , customer_last_name , customer_middle_initial FROM Customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Orders)

results.question: What are the id, name, price and color of the products which have not been ordered for at least twice?
results.gold_sql: SELECT product_id ,  product_name ,  product_price ,  product_color FROM Products EXCEPT SELECT T1.product_id ,  T1.product_name ,  T1.product_price ,  T1.product_color FROM Products AS T1 JOIN Order_items AS T2 ON T1.product_id  =  T2.product_id JOIN Orders AS T3 ON T2.order_id  =  T3.order_id GROUP BY T1.product_id HAVING count(*)  >=  2	e_commerce
results.baseline_sql: SELECT T1.product_id , T1.product_name , T1.product_price , T1.product_color FROM Products AS T1 WHERE T1.product_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.product_id FROM Order_Items AS T2 GROUP BY T2.product_id HAVING count(*) >= 2)
results.decomposition_plan:
1. Tables: 
   - Products
   - Order_Items

2. Join path: 
   - Inner join on Order_Items.product_id = Products.product_id

3. Filters: 
   - Order_Items.order_id is not in the subquery of order_ids that appear at least twice

4. Aggregation / grouping: 
   - Group by product_id

5. Ordering / limit: 
   - None

6. Subqueries / set operations: 
   - Subquery to find order_ids that appear at least twice
results.decomposition_sql: SELECT T1.product_id , T1.product_name , T1.product_price , T1.product_color FROM Products AS T1 INNER JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.product_id = T2.product_id WHERE T2.order_id NOT IN (SELECT order_id FROM Order_Items GROUP BY order_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) >= 2)
